# A Randomised Trial of Drug X in Stable COPD

## Abstract
We conducted a double-blind randomised controlled trial of Drug X versus placebo in 240
patients with stable COPD. The primary outcome was change in FEV1 at 12 weeks.

## Methods
Participants were randomised 1:1. The sample size was determined to detect a 100 mL difference
in FEV1 with 80% power. Ethics approval was obtained and all participants gave written consent.

## Results
Drug X improved FEV1 by 120 mL (95% CI 80–160) versus placebo. Adverse events were balanced
between arms.

## Discussion
These findings suggest a modest benefit. Limitations include the short follow-up and a
single-centre design.
